name: Beverly Hills
seoTitle: Landscaping in Beverly Hills | Living Colors
metaDescription: Premium landscape design-build for Beverly Hills estates, from the flats to Trousdale. Licensed, insured, and detail-obsessed. Start your free guided quote today.
h1: Estate Landscape Design & Build in Beverly Hills
heroSubhead: Grounds worthy of the address, from the palm-lined flats to the hillside estates above Sunset.
intro: Beverly Hills sets a standard that few neighborhoods anywhere can match, and the landscaping has to answer for it. Whether your property sits on the gracious flats south of Sunset, along the winding drives of the hills, or high in Trousdale Estates, the grounds are read as an extension of the home itself. We design gardens that carry that weight with restraint, marrying formal structure and living color into something that feels effortless.

Much of the city's residential fabric is a conversation between period architecture and mature landscape, Spanish Revival, Regency, and clean mid-century lines framed by hedging, specimen trees, and considered lighting. We work in that dialogue rather than over it. From privacy screening along busy corridors to motor-court entries and rear gardens built for entertaining, every element is planned to age gracefully and photograph beautifully in every season.

localContext.title: Trousdale hillside grades
localContext.body: The elevated lots of Trousdale and the streets above Sunset come with slope, drainage, and view considerations that flat-lot design never faces. We engineer terracing, retaining, and planting that stabilizes grade while keeping those coveted city-to-ocean sightlines wide open.

localContext.title: Privacy on the flats
localContext.body: On the celebrated flats, generous lots sit close to well-traveled streets. Layered ficus and podocarpus hedging, mature specimen placement, and thoughtful gated entries create genuine seclusion without turning the front elevation into a wall, preserving the neighborhood's open, gracious character.

localContext.title: Estate-level water strategy
localContext.body: Signature lawns and lush borders still define the Beverly Hills look, but they must coexist with California water rules. We blend smart drip irrigation, weather-based controllers, and drought-resilient palettes so gardens stay verdant and compliant through long, dry summers.

faqs.q: Do you handle steep hillside lots in the Beverly Hills hills?
faqs.a: Yes. Hillside properties above Sunset and throughout Trousdale are a core part of our work. We coordinate terracing, retaining walls, drainage, and slope-appropriate planting so your grounds are both stable and stunning, with view corridors carefully preserved throughout the design.

faqs.q: Can you create real privacy without a fortress look?
faqs.a: Absolutely. We favor layered living screens, mature ficus, podocarpus, and specimen trees, combined with grade changes and elegant entry design. The result is genuine seclusion from the street that still reads as gracious and open, in keeping with the flats' architectural character.

faqs.q: How do you keep a lush look under water restrictions?
faqs.a: We design climate-smart palettes that read as lush while using far less water, pairing select lawns with drought-tolerant borders, high-efficiency drip, and weather-based controllers. You get the verdant Beverly Hills aesthetic that also holds up to the region's ongoing conservation requirements.

faqs.q: How does the free quote work?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard is a guided intake that takes about ninety seconds. You share your address, goals, and style preferences, and we follow up to schedule a walkthrough. It costs nothing and gives us the context to tailor recommendations to your specific property.

name: Bel Air
seoTitle: Landscaping in Bel Air | Living Colors Landscape
metaDescription: Design-build landscapes for Bel Air's canyon estates and gated compounds. Fire-wise, view-driven, HOA-savvy. Begin your free guided quote in about 90 seconds.
h1: Canyon & Estate Landscaping in Bel Air
heroSubhead: Landscapes shaped for Bel Air's canyons, gated drives, and long ocean-and-city views.
intro: Tucked into the Santa Monica Mountains, Bel Air is a world of winding canyon roads, private gates, and estates that seem to grow out of the ridgelines. Landscaping here is rarely simple: lots roll, drop, and climb, and the best gardens work with that topography instead of fighting it. We design grounds that feel discovered rather than installed, where terraces, water features, and specimen planting settle naturally into the hillside.

The community's architecture ranges from stately traditional and Mediterranean compounds to bold contemporary builds, and each demands a different landscape vocabulary. Behind the gates of East Gate, West Gate, and the upper canyons, we plan for privacy, arrival sequences, and outdoor rooms that take full advantage of the elevation. Every project also accounts for the realities of canyon living, from wildfire exposure to slope stability and the mountains' distinct microclimate.

localContext.title: Fire-wise defensible space
localContext.body: Bel Air's canyon setting places many homes in high fire-hazard zones. We design defensible-space landscapes that meet brush-clearance requirements, favoring low-fuel, high-moisture plantings and hardscape buffers near structures without sacrificing the lush, layered look the neighborhood is known for.

localContext.title: Slope stability & drainage
localContext.body: Steep canyon lots demand serious engineering below the beauty. We integrate retaining systems, proper subsurface drainage, and deep-rooting slope planting to manage runoff and erosion through winter storms, protecting both your investment and the hillsides that give Bel Air its character.

localContext.title: View-corridor design
localContext.body: Elevation is Bel Air's great luxury, and framing those city, canyon, and ocean views is central to good design. We position terraces, pools, and low plantings to preserve sightlines, using structure and lighting to draw the eye outward rather than blocking the horizon.

faqs.q: Can you design fire-wise landscaping for a canyon property?
faqs.a: Yes, and in Bel Air it's essential. We create defensible-space plans that satisfy brush-clearance rules while keeping your grounds beautiful, using low-fuel, high-moisture plant palettes, strategic hardscape buffers, and irrigation zoned to keep the perimeter green through the dry season.

faqs.q: How do you handle steep, unstable slopes?
faqs.a: Slope work is foundational to Bel Air projects. We combine retaining structures, engineered drainage, and deep-rooting groundcovers to control erosion and runoff. The result manages winter storms and protects your hillside while still delivering the terraced, layered gardens the canyons are famous for.

faqs.q: Do you coordinate with HOA and architectural review boards?
faqs.a: We do. Several Bel Air enclaves and gated communities have architectural committees with specific guidelines. We prepare submittal-ready plans and manage the review process so your project moves forward smoothly and stays fully compliant from concept through final installation.

faqs.q: What does the free guided quote involve?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard walks you through a short, roughly ninety-second intake covering your location, goals, and aesthetic. From there we arrange an on-site walkthrough. There's no cost or obligation, and it helps us tailor ideas to your canyon lot's specific slope, exposure, and views.

name: Brentwood
metaDescription: Landscape design-build for Brentwood's canyons, Country Club, and flats. Timeless gardens, outdoor living, and smart irrigation. Get a free guided quote in minutes.
h1: Refined Landscape Design & Build in Brentwood
heroSubhead: Gardens with quiet confidence for Brentwood's leafy canyons and gracious family estates.
intro: Brentwood carries an understated kind of prestige, tree-lined streets, established gardens, and homes that value comfort and craftsmanship over spectacle. From the family-friendly flats near San Vicente to the secluded reaches of Mandeville and Kenter Canyon, the neighborhood rewards landscapes that feel settled and warm. We design grounds that suit this sensibility: mature planting, generous outdoor living, and materials that only look better with time.

The area's building stock leans toward traditional, Cape Cod, and transitional architecture, often set on deep lots framed by heritage trees. Our work respects those anchors, weaving new gardens around existing canopies and grade. Whether you're refreshing a Country Club-area estate or reimagining a canyon hillside, we balance privacy, play, and entertaining while planning for Brentwood's coastal-influenced climate and the region's ongoing water considerations.

localContext.title: Canyon slope & fire exposure
localContext.body: Mandeville, Kenter, and Sullivan Canyon homes sit in hillside terrain with real wildfire exposure. We design for slope stability and defensible space together, combining engineered drainage, erosion-control planting, and low-fuel perimeters so canyon properties stay both secure and beautifully green.

localContext.title: Heritage tree preservation
localContext.body: Brentwood's deep lots are often defined by mature oaks, sycamores, and towering pines. We design around these anchors, protecting root zones during construction and shaping gardens that complement existing canopy rather than competing with it, keeping the neighborhood's established, shaded character intact.

localContext.title: Family-focused outdoor living
localContext.body: Many Brentwood households want grounds that work hard, pools, play lawns, dining terraces, and quiet corners all on one lot. We zone spaces thoughtfully so entertaining, recreation, and relaxation coexist, using durable, low-maintenance materials suited to active family life.

faqs.q: Do you work on Mandeville and Kenter Canyon hillside lots?
faqs.a: Regularly. Brentwood's canyons bring slope, drainage, and fire considerations that we address as a package, engineered retaining and drainage, erosion-control planting, and defensible-space perimeters. You get a hillside garden that's stable and fire-aware without losing the lush, layered look these canyons deserve.

faqs.q: How do you protect existing oaks and mature trees?
faqs.a: We treat established trees as the backbone of the design. That means protecting root zones during construction, adjusting grading and irrigation to their needs, and shaping planting around their canopy. Preserving Brentwood's heritage trees keeps your property's shade, value, and settled character.

faqs.q: Can one design serve both kids and elegant entertaining?
faqs.a: Yes, and it's one of our favorite challenges. We zone the property so play lawns, pools, dining terraces, and quiet retreats each have their place. Durable materials and smart layout let a Brentwood garden handle everyday family life and refined gatherings equally well.

faqs.q: How do I get started with a quote?
faqs.a: Use our Smart Quote Wizard, a guided intake that takes roughly ninety seconds. Share your property details, priorities, and style, and we'll follow up to schedule a walkthrough. It's completely free and gives us what we need to make tailored, relevant recommendations.

name: Pacific Palisades
seoTitle: Landscaping in Pacific Palisades | Living Colors
metaDescription: Coastal-canyon landscape design-build for Pacific Palisades, from the Riviera to the bluffs. Fire-wise and salt-tolerant. Start your free guided quote in 90 seconds.
h1: Coastal & Canyon Landscaping in Pacific Palisades
heroSubhead: Gardens tuned to the Palisades, where ocean breezes, canyon slopes, and coastal light meet.
intro: Pacific Palisades lives between the mountains and the sea, and its landscapes should feel that way. From the elegant streets of the Riviera to the bluff-top lots above the coast and the wooded pockets of the Highlands, this is a place of ocean views, canyon greenery, and a relaxed coastal formality. We design grounds that embrace the setting, framing the water, softening the hillsides, and creating outdoor rooms made for the Palisades' famously gentle climate.

The community blends traditional, Mediterranean, and coastal-contemporary homes, many perched on slopes or edged by canyon and open space. That beauty comes with responsibility: salt-laden air, fire-prone terrain, and grade that demands careful engineering. We plan for all of it, choosing resilient plant palettes, stabilizing slopes, and building hardscape and lighting that stand up to the marine environment while looking effortless year after year.

localContext.title: Coastal salt-air resilience
localContext.body: Proximity to the ocean means salt-laden wind that punishes soft plantings and corrodes lesser materials. We specify salt-tolerant, wind-hardy species and marine-grade fixtures and finishes, so Palisades gardens near the bluffs and coast stay lush and structurally sound for the long term.

localContext.title: Fire-wise canyon edges
localContext.body: Homes bordering Temescal, Rustic Canyon, and the Highlands sit against fire-prone open space. We design defensible-space landscapes that meet brush-clearance standards, using low-fuel plantings, irrigated green buffers, and hardscape transitions to protect structures without a bare, institutional look.

localContext.title: Slope & bluff stability
localContext.body: The Palisades' dramatic grades, from Riviera hillsides to coastal bluffs, require real geotechnical respect. We integrate retaining, drainage, and deep-rooting slope planting to manage runoff and erosion, keeping both your gardens and the ground beneath them secure through wet winters.

faqs.q: Which plants hold up to Palisades ocean air?
faqs.a: We lean on salt-tolerant, wind-hardy species, think coastal natives, hardy Mediterranean shrubs, and select ornamental grasses, that thrive in marine conditions. Paired with marine-grade hardscape and lighting finishes, these palettes keep bluff-side and coastal gardens looking lush without constant salt-damage repairs.

faqs.q: Do you handle fire-wise landscaping near the canyons?
faqs.a: Yes. Properties along Rustic Canyon, Temescal, and the Highlands need defensible space. We design brush-clearance-compliant landscapes with low-fuel plantings, irrigated green buffers, and hardscape transitions near structures, protecting your home while preserving the wooded, canyon-edge beauty that defines the Palisades.

faqs.q: Can you stabilize a steep bluff or hillside lot?
faqs.a: We specialize in it. Palisades grades, from Riviera slopes to coastal bluffs, call for engineered retaining, thorough drainage, and deep-rooting slope planting. We manage erosion and runoff so your terraces, gardens, and views stay secure and intact through the region's wet winters.

faqs.q: How does the free guided quote work?
faqs.a: Our Smart Quote Wizard is a quick, roughly ninety-second guided intake. You tell us about your property, goals, and style, and we schedule a walkthrough from there. It's free and no-obligation, and it lets us tailor ideas to your coastal exposure, slope, and views.

name: Malibu
seoTitle: Landscaping in Malibu | Living Colors Landscape
metaDescription: Beachfront and hillside landscape design-build across Malibu, from the Colony to the canyons. Salt-tough, fire-wise, view-first. Get a free guided quote in 90 seconds.
heroSubhead: Landscapes built for Malibu's realities, ocean spray, canyon fire country, and 27 miles of coast.
intro: Malibu is defined by extremes, oceanfront on one side, rugged Santa Monica Mountains on the other, and a ribbon of coast connecting Point Dume, the Colony, and the canyons between. Landscaping here is as much about resilience as beauty. Whether your home fronts the sand or climbs a chaparral hillside, we design grounds that stand up to the elements while capturing the light, the breeze, and those unmatched Pacific views.

The look ranges from breezy coastal-modern to rustic ranch and Mediterranean, and the site conditions are unforgiving: relentless salt air on the beach, wildfire pressure in the canyons, and slopes that demand engineering. We meet those challenges head-on with salt-tolerant palettes, defensible-space planning, and stabilized terrain, then layer in the outdoor living, lighting, and lush color that make a Malibu property feel like the retreat it should be.

localContext.title: Beachfront salt & wind
localContext.body: Oceanfront lots along the Colony, Broad Beach, and Point Dume face constant salt spray and wind. We build with marine-grade materials and salt-tolerant, wind-firm planting so gardens and hardscape resist corrosion and dieback, keeping beachfront grounds handsome despite the harsh coastal exposure.

localContext.title: Canyon wildfire & defensible space
localContext.body: Malibu's canyons and hillsides are true fire country. We design defensible-space landscapes that meet strict brush-clearance codes, using low-fuel plantings, irrigated green zones, and hardscape buffers to shield structures while keeping the natural, chaparral-framed character these hillside properties are prized for.

localContext.title: Coastal permitting & slopes
localContext.body: Much of Malibu falls under Coastal Commission oversight, and hillside lots add slope and drainage complexity. We plan projects with these constraints in mind, coordinating permit-ready designs and engineered slope stabilization so your landscape is both approvable and built to endure.

faqs.q: What survives on a Malibu beachfront lot?
faqs.a: Salt-tolerant, wind-firm species are the answer, coastal natives, hardy succulents, and resilient Mediterranean plants, paired with marine-grade hardscape and lighting. On the Colony, Broad Beach, or Point Dume, this approach keeps gardens lush and structures corrosion-resistant despite constant salt spray and ocean wind.

faqs.q: How do you make a canyon property fire-wise?
faqs.a: Malibu's canyons demand serious defensible space. We design to strict brush-clearance codes using low-fuel plant palettes, irrigated green buffer zones, and hardscape transitions near the home. The result protects the structure while keeping the chaparral-framed, natural look that makes hillside Malibu so special.

faqs.q: Do you deal with Coastal Commission permitting?
faqs.a: Yes. Many Malibu properties fall under Coastal Commission jurisdiction, which shapes what's possible near the shore and on the bluffs. We design with those requirements in mind and prepare permit-ready plans, helping your project move through review while still achieving an ambitious, beautiful result.

faqs.q: What's the first step to getting a design?
faqs.a: Start with our Smart Quote Wizard, a guided intake that takes about ninety seconds. Share your location, goals, and style preferences, and we'll arrange a site walkthrough. It's free and carries no obligation, and it helps us tailor recommendations to your specific coast or canyon setting.
